Title: Development of ''Purple Endosperm Rice'' by Engineering Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in the Endosperm with a High-Efficiency Transgene Stacking System

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in developing PER endosperm; low in WT endosperm
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous OsWD40 was upregulated in PER endosperm. 2. It likely partnered with transgenic ZmLc and ZmPl to rebuild the MBW complex. 3. This endogenous component contributed to activation of anthocyanin-related genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Endogenous cofactor that can be leveraged with introduced MYB/bHLH regulators during anthocyanin engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not itself transformed; endogenous gene recruited into engineered regulatory complex
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports anthocyanin biosynthesis through MBW complex formation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data referenced; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR; inference from MBW model in PER lines

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (distinct rice ESP used for each transgene; exact promoter for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) in PER line; absent in WT endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; genotype-phenotype comparison of lines lacking SsCHS
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, the entry step of flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of full anthocyanin pathway stacks for endosperm biofortification; should be combined with regulatory genes and downstream structural genes rather than used alone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHS was one of six Coleus structural genes introduced to reconstruct anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Lines lacking SsCHS together with SsCHI and SsF3'H showed lighter purple grains, indicating these transgenes enhance anthocyanin accumulation. 3. Its function complements weak/silent endogenous endosperm pathway activity.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase, chalcone/stilbene synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of multigene stack associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines, though gene-specific contribution not isolated
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core upstream precursor pool for anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data referenced for endogenous rice endosperm genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in engineered rice endosperm; absence lowers purple pigmentation intensity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text refers to SsF3OH/F3'H formatting inconsistently due to typography; SsCHS specifically contributed as exogenous supplement despite endogenous OsCHS activation

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm of PER line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; RT-PCR; HPLC; genotype-phenotype comparison of lines lacking SsCHI
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcone to flavanone (naringenin-type intermediate) in anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable in stacked anthocyanin engineering to improve flux through early flavonoid pathway in cereal endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHI was included among the six structural genes required for engineering anthocyanin synthesis in rice endosperm. 2. PER lines lacking SsCHI together with SsCHS and SsF3'H showed reduced purple coloration and anthocyanin content. 3. The transgene enhanced pathway output beyond activation of endogenous genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of stack, associated with reduced grain weight at line level; gene-specific effect NA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms essential early intermediate toward anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous rice genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports increased anthocyanin accumulation; absence contributes to lighter purple grains
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsCHI could be activated, but exogenous SsCHI still enhanced endosperm anthocyanin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm of PER line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; RT-PCR; anthocyanin accumulation in PER
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanone to dihydroflavonol in the anthocyanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical complementing gene where endogenous OsF3H was not activated in endosperm; should be included in engineering stacks
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3H was transferred because endogenous OsF3H was not activated in PER endosperm. 2. It helped complete the anthocyanin pathway in rice endosperm. 3. Its inclusion was necessary for successful production of purple endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms obligatory mid-pathway precursor for anthocyanidin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous rice genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables anthocyanin biosynthesis in endosperm by complementing inactive endogenous step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Figure 5 notes endogenous OsF3H was not activated by regulatory genes in WT or PER

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm of PER line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; RT-PCR; anthocyanin HPLC; comparison of lines lacking SsF3'H
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B-ring at the 3' position, directing flux toward cyanidin-type anthocyanidins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for cyanidin/peonidin-producing branch engineering in cereals; can be used to steer anthocyanin composition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3'H was one of the introduced structural genes enabling anthocyanin formation in rice endosperm. 2. Lines missing SsF3'H together with SsCHS and SsCHI had lighter purple grains, showing it enhances pathway flux and/or composition. 3. It supports cyanidin-derived anthocyanin production, matching observed C3G and P3G products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directs branch flux toward cyanidin-derived anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous rice genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances accumulation of cyanidin/peonidin-type anthocyanins in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Typography in paper renders gene as SsF3OH or SsF3'OH/F30H; function clearly corresponds to flavonoid 3'-hydroxylase

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm of PER line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; RT-PCR; loss-of-gene correlation in line H#12 lacking SsDFR; HPLC anthocyanin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential target for anthocyanin biofortification where endogenous DFR is defective; prime gene for complementation in rice endosperm engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous OsDFR is defective in the recipient rice varieties. 2. Introduction of SsDFR complemented this blocked step and was required for purple endosperm formation. 3. A line lacking SsDFR failed to produce purple seeds, providing strong functional evidence.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous rice genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for anthocyanin accumulation; absence abolishes purple endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the clearest causal genes in the study because endogenous rice ortholog is defective and transgene loss eliminates phenotype

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm of PER line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transgenic expression; RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin detection
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins such as cyanidin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary late-pathway gene for reconstructing anthocyanin biosynthesis in endosperm, especially where endogenous OsANS is not expressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsANS was introduced because endogenous OsANS was not activated in PER endosperm. 2. It completed the late anthocyanin pathway and enabled production of anthocyanidins. 3. Together with decorating endogenous genes, it supported C3G and P3G accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms anthocyanidin backbone of target anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous rice genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables anthocyanin production in engineered endosperm by complementing silent endogenous step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsANS remained unactivated in PER according to Figure 5

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing PER endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis genes and related upstream phenylalanine pathway genes; OsWD40 upregulated; with ZmPl activates OsCHS; OsCHI; OsF3'H and other endogenous anthocyanin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms rebuilt MBW complex with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data referenced for endogenous endosperm genes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transformation; semi-quantitative RT-PCR of transgenes and endogenous targets; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; genotype-phenotype analysis of lines lacking ZmLc
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required positive regulator for anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; absence results in white grains
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains, confirming its requirement
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key bHLH regulator for rebuilding anthocyanin regulatory network in cereal endosperm; should be paired with a MYB partner and structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis, decoration, transport, and activation of upstream phenylalanine pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLc was introduced as a maize bHLH regulator to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Together with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40, it rebuilt an MBW complex that activated or enhanced 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes. 3. Lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains, demonstrating that it is essential for engineered anthocyanin production.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/helps activate multiple endogenous anthocyanin biosynthetic, decorating, transporter, and upstream phenylalanine pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator required to rebuild regulatory competence of anthocyanin pathway in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PER lines showed ~25% reduced grain weight overall, though specific contribution of ZmLc alone not separated

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing PER endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis genes; OsWD40 upregulated; activation/enhancement of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes in cooperation with ZmLc
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms rebuilt MBW complex with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data referenced for endogenous genes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transformation; RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator enabling anthocyanin accumulation in engineered rice endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen instead of ZmC1 because ZmPl reportedly has stronger activity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful MYB regulator for anthocyanin engineering in cereal endosperm; best deployed with bHLH partner and structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and associated decoration/transport gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPl was selected as a strong maize MYB regulator to activate anthocyanin pathway genes in rice endosperm. 2. Together with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40, it rebuilt the MBW complex and activated or enhanced endogenous anthocyanin-related genes. 3. Its expression contributed to successful accumulation of C3G and P3G in rice endosperm.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/helps activate endogenous anthocyanin biosynthetic, decorating, transporter, and upstream phenylalanine pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator needed to re-establish anthocyanin regulatory network in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PER lines had reduced grain weight overall; gene-specific effect NA
